input.title: Multiselect
input.body:
Add a new multiselect mode that allows multiple thoughts to be selected and modified at once. Activating a command while more than one thought is selected should typically execute the command on each selected thought.

## Requirements

- [x] On desktop, hold Cmd (OSX) or Ctrl (PC) and click on a thought to add/remove it from the current selection.
- [x] On mobile, long press a thought and release to select/deselect it.
- [x] On mobile, ‚Üê‚Üì‚Üí to select the cursor and all its siblings. Change `subcategorizeAll` to ‚Üê‚Üì‚Üí‚Üê‚Üë. 
- [x] Highlight the bullet of each thought that is selected. Use the same style as a thought being dragged.
- [x] Perma alert "1 thought selected".
- [x] ~~Change alert close button to "cancel".~~
- [x] When the last selected thought is deselected, or a command is executed, close the alert. 
- [x] Cancel when a drag is initiated.
- [x] ~~Combine with drag board.~~

## Commands

The following shortcuts should be executed on each selected thought. The selection should be preserved after execution. All shortcuts not listed below or in Special Cases should show an alert like "Cannot subcategorize multiple thoughts" when activated with multiple thoughts selected.

- archive
- collapseContext
- indent
- moveThoughtDown
- moveThoughtUp
- outdent
- pin
- pinAll
- proseView
- splitSentences
- toggleContextView
- toggleSort
- toggleTableView

### Special Cases

- **cursorDown** - If activated with no modifiers, should move the cursor as usual and cancel multiselect. If Shift is held down, should add the sibling after the last selected thought to the selection.
- **cursorUp** - If activated with no modifiers, should move the cursor as usual and cancel multiselect. If Shift is held down, should remove last selected thought from the selection.
- **subcategorizeOne** / **subcategorizeAll** - `subcategorizeOne` and `subcategorizeAll` should do the same thing when multiple thoughts are selected. It should move the selected thoughts to the same empty thought:
   e.g. If `b` and `c` are selected:
   ```
   - a
   - b
   - c
   - d
   ```
   Then activating either `subcategorizeOne` or `subcategorizeAll` should result in:
   ```
   - a
   - [empty]
     - b
     - c
   - d
   ```
- **copyCursor** / **exportContext** - Export multiple root thoughts.
   e.g. If `a` and `c` are selected:
   ```
   - a
     - b
   - c
     - d
   - e
     - f
   ```
   Then export:
   ```
   - a
     - b
   - c
     - d
   ```
   If an ancestor and its descendant is selected, only export the ancestor.
